project.name: GateGrave Bot System
project.current_focus: Modular subsystems with in-memory managers, event-style flows, and automated tests

architecture_rules: Discord gateway translates input into events only (no gameplay logic).
Systems should not call each other directly for gameplay outcomes.
Persistent player/world data belongs in World State.
Combat instances are isolated and in-memory.
Snapshot/recovery modules exist per subsystem and should stay separated.

folder_layout.apps/controller/src: Event router and system routing stubs
folder_layout.apps/combat-system/src: Combat engine modules and testing harness
folder_layout.apps/dungeon-exploration/src: Dungeon/session models, movement, challenges, snapshots, harness

apps/world-system/src.world-events: World events, world boss, seasonal events

guild: Shared storage flow with duplicate withdrawal protection
world_events: Seasonal event system with active window, participation gating, and retirement
hunter_association: Contract/hunt board flow: create, claim, cancel, complete, expire
ranking: Ranking types: hunter, guild, dungeon_leaderboard, world_event_contribution
Score update with optional history/audit trail
advanced_systems: Snapshot and restore for guild, guild storage, raid, world events, contracts, ranking references

entry_points.apps/world-system/src/index.js: Exports all world modules and helpers
entry_points.subsystem_index_pattern: Each subsystem uses index.js with default managers and wrappers

chatgpt_bootstrap_prompt: You are helping on the GateGrave Bot System Node.js monorepo. Follow event-driven architecture rules: gateway has no gameplay logic, systems do not directly call each other for gameplay, and state layers remain separated. Work primarily in apps/world-system/src modular subsystems (guild, raid, world-events, hunter-association, ranking, advanced-systems) and preserve existing in-memory manager/test patterns. Add beginner-readable JavaScript with structured result payloads and matching automated tests in each subsystem's testing/ folder.
contributor_notes: Keep new logic modular by subsystem.
Add or extend tests for each phase increment.
Prefer structured { ok, event_type, payload } flow results.
Keep snapshot modules isolated by domain.
